instruction: Change the return type from `list` to `tuple`. The function must return a two-element tuple `(result, removed_count)` where `result` is the deduplicated list (same logic as the original: collapse adjacent duplicates, preserve order) and `removed_count` is the total number of elements removed (i.e., `len(items) - len(result)`). For an empty input, return `([], 0)`. All other deduplication behaviour (collapsing adjacent duplicates, preserving order) must remain unchanged.
